note: The crafts of the Quickwood/Palelands Reach (death_life axis). Vivimancy is the life-pole tradition, Palework the death-pole. Learned by traveling the Reach or training under Rootkin/Ashwardens — NOT available at Valley creation; gated like precursor (learned). Higher tiers pull the wielder's own pole-intensity toward their pole (a Vivimancer drifts life-hot, a Paleworker death-hot) — the geography writes itself onto the practitioner. axes use signed values for engine compat. Authored by Aevi (PO), SNG-016 skill build-out.

abilities.name: Quickening
abilities.narrationHints: Life poured where life was thin — a wound closing, a seed bursting, a dying thing pulled back toward the quick.
abilities.description: Accelerate life: heal, grow, revive the nearly-gone. The Rootkin's first craft.
abilities.notFor: Raising the truly dead (only the nearly-gone); creating life from nothing; halting decay that has finished.

tree.name: Green Touch
tree.grants: Close wounds and speed healing on the living; coax rapid growth in plants; steady a failing pulse.
tree.cannot: Nothing past death's threshold; overuse tires the wielder faster than it heals.

tree.name: The Quick Return
tree.grants: Pull the nearly-dead back if reached in time; regrow what was lost (within reason); make barren ground bloom.
tree.cannot: A return has a cost the GM names; the regrown is not always the same as the lost.

tree.name: Unstoppable Spring
tree.grants: Life at flood — mass healing, land quickened for a season, a body remade toward health. The Heartroot's power in miniature.
tree.cannot: It does not know when to stop — quickening can grow a thing past its right shape; the wielder drifts life-hot, and life that will not stop is its own horror (see the Quiet in the Quickwood).

abilities.name: Palework
abilities.narrationHints: The mercy and the weight of endings — a thing allowed to stop, a rot read like a map, a stillness that is not cruelty.
abilities.description: The craft of endings: ease death, read decay, still what should be still. The Ashwardens' art — not killing, but tending the end.
abilities.notFor: Murder (Palework eases ends, it does not force the living to die); necromancy/animation; stopping a death that must happen.

tree.name: The Easing
tree.grants: Ease a dying thing's passing; read how and how long ago something died; grant true rest to what cannot find it.
tree.cannot: Cannot kill the healthy; cannot bring back; the easing is a mercy, refused if unwilling.

tree.name: Reading the Rot
tree.grants: Read decay like text — what killed a thing, what a ruin died of, what a blight will do next; halt a spreading rot by giving it a clean end.
tree.cannot: Reading is not undoing; some rots refuse a clean end.

tree.name: The Long Stillness
tree.grants: Grant endings at scale — a battlefield eased, a plague stopped by stilling its front, a thing that should have died a century ago finally allowed to. The mercy the life-Reach cannot give itself.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts death-hot; the Stillness is powerful enough to be mistaken for a weapon, and the mistake is fatal to trust; never raises, never forces.

abilities.name: Deathsense
abilities.narrationHints: A quiet knowing of what has died, is dying, or is near its end nearby.
abilities.description: Sense death and dying around you — the recently-dead, the failing, the near-gone.
abilities.notFor: Causing death; seeing the future of a death.

tree.name: Deathsense
tree.grants: Feel where a thing has died or is dying nearby; judge how long ago; sense a life near its end.
tree.cannot: Reads presence, not cause; the healthy hide nothing to find.

tree.name: The Long Count
tree.grants: Sense not only that a thing is dying but HOW, and roughly how long it has — the shape of the ending, not just its fact.
tree.cannot: Knowing the hour does not give you the power to move it, and it will not let you unknow it.

tree.name: The Hour Known
tree.grants: The ending stands plain: its cause, its hour, and what would turn it aside — if anything would.
tree.cannot: Some endings have no turning-aside, and the Ashwardens hold that seeing that clearly is the whole of the craft.

abilities.name: Lifesense
abilities.narrationHints: The warmth of the living felt like heat through a wall — who is near, how strong their spark.
abilities.description: Sense living things nearby — number, vigor, hidden presence.
abilities.notFor: Reading thoughts; distinguishing individuals by identity.

tree.name: Lifesense
tree.grants: Feel living things through walls and dark; judge their vigor; notice one hiding.
tree.cannot: Vigor, not identity; the barely-alive read faint.

tree.name: The Green Read
tree.grants: Read the whole living field of a place — what is thriving, what is failing, what is merely holding on.
tree.cannot: It reports vitality, not virtue. A thriving thing may be thriving on something you love.

tree.name: The Whole Living Field
tree.grants: Feel every living thing for a great distance as one continuous fabric, and feel where it is torn.
tree.cannot: You cannot stop feeling it. Rootkin at this depth grieve forests the way others grieve people.

abilities.name: Staunch
abilities.narrationHints: A hand pressed and a word, and the bleeding slows to a stop.
abilities.description: Field-stop bleeding and stabilize the badly hurt — the first, portable mercy of Vivimancy.
abilities.notFor: Closing deep wounds fully (that's Quickening); reviving the gone.

tree.name: Staunch
tree.grants: Stop bleeding, stabilize a crashing body, buy time for real healing.
tree.cannot: Buys time; does not mend; the already-dead are past it.

tree.name: The Held Wound
tree.grants: Hold a mortal wound closed by will — buy hours where there were minutes.
tree.cannot: Held is not healed. When you let go, the wound resumes exactly where it was.

tree.name: The Refused Bleed
tree.grants: Refuse a death outright at the moment of bleeding — the body stops losing what it cannot spare.
tree.cannot: Refusal costs you what it saves them; the Rootkin pay it gladly and it still costs.

abilities.name: Wither
abilities.narrationHints: Touch, and the quick goes out of a thing — a vine, a lock's wooden pin, a reaching hand.
abilities.description: Hasten decay in a thing — rot wood, wilt growth, weaken the living by drawing the quick out. Palework's edge turned outward.
abilities.notFor: Killing a person outright (drains, does not slay); undoing what's withered.

tree.name: Wither
tree.grants: Rot or wilt a plant, a wooden thing, a small living obstacle; weaken a living foe's grip or vigor by a touch.
tree.cannot: Slow against a strong will; the Ashwardens frown on it turned toward people.

tree.name: The Grey Touch
tree.grants: Age and fail a thing by touch — a rope, a door, a wound made suddenly older than it was.
tree.cannot: Withering is not killing; it is hastening. It cannot hasten what was not already going that way.

tree.name: The Withering
tree.grants: Wither at range and at scale — a field, a barricade, a charge of enemies who suddenly feel their years.
tree.cannot: The Ashwardens permit this only against what should end. Used otherwise it marks you, visibly and permanently.

abilities.name: Graftlife
abilities.narrationHints: What was lost is coaxed to grow back — not the same, but living.
abilities.description: Regrow lost tissue, mend a maiming, coax a broken living thing back toward whole. Mid-tier Vivimancy.
abilities.notFor: Perfect restoration (the regrown differs); regrowing the dead.

tree.name: Graftlife
tree.grants: Regrow a lost finger, mend a crippling wound over time, restore a blighted field to growth.
tree.cannot: The regrown is never quite the lost; costs the wielder deeply.

tree.name: The Grafted Limb
tree.grants: Graft living tissue that was not there — replace what was lost with what still grows.
tree.cannot: The graft is not the original, and it knows it. The body argues with it for a long time.

tree.name: Rootwork
tree.grants: Graft life into what was not alive, or was no longer — bone to wood, a heart to a stopped chest.
tree.cannot: This is the near edge of the Rootkin's own taboo. What comes back is alive; whether it is the same is another question.

abilities.name: Death-Ward
abilities.narrationHints: A sign laid down that the dying may not be disturbed — and the restless dead may not pass.
abilities.description: Inscribe a ward against death's disturbances — keep the restless-dead out, or keep a passing peaceful and unbroken. Palework as protection.
abilities.notFor: Preventing a natural death; harming the living.

tree.name: Death-Ward
tree.grants: Ward a space so a death goes undisturbed, or so what should stay dead cannot rise or reach in.
tree.cannot: Wards the threshold, not the outcome; a strong enough wrongness strains it.

tree.name: The Turned Hour
tree.grants: Turn a death aside — not heal the wound, but refuse the hour, and make it come later.
tree.cannot: Later is not never; the hour you turned is still owed, and it remembers the way.

tree.name: Not Today
tree.grants: Stand between a person and their ending and simply refuse it, wholly, for as long as you can hold.
tree.cannot: You are holding it with your own weight. The Ashwardens say the ward that never breaks has not yet met its hour.

abilities.name: The Last Gift
abilities.narrationHints: At the very edge, the dying are given one clear hour — lucid, painless, theirs.
abilities.description: Grant a dying being a final lucid, painless interval to say and do what remains — the co-firing craft, life and death held together in mercy.
abilities.notFor: Extending life (it eases the end, not delays it); forcing lucidity on the unwilling.

tree.name: The Last Gift
tree.grants: Give the dying a clear, painless final interval — words said, peace made, the end met whole.
tree.cannot: Does not postpone death; the gift is the hour, not more hours.
